title: CUHK Unveils VLM with Spatial Intelligence for Advanced AI Robotics
summary: CUHK researchers developed a Vision-Language Model integrated with spatial intelligence to enable robots to perform complex manipulation tasks autonomously.
contents:
# CUHK Unveils VLM with Spatial Intelligence for Advanced AI Robotics

## Overview
CUHK researchers developed a Vision-Language Model integrated with spatial intelligence to enable robots to perform complex manipulation tasks autonomously.

## Details
The team from CUHK's Faculty of Engineering created a scalable framework supporting visuo-tactile fusion for precise spatial instructions and dexterous manipulation on humanoid platforms. Led by Professor Dou Qi from the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, the breakthrough allows comprehension of 3D spatial information like humans and enhances AI analytical capabilities. Findings got published in Science Robotics, demonstrating applications in long-horizon tasks with various objects and tactile feedback for adaptive grasping.

## Location
Hong Kong

## Source Context
Reporting referenced from coverage tracked by the @vantortech OSINT beat. The cited outlet provides additional context, attribution, and verification of the claims summarized above.

## Tags
GEOINT, spatial intelligence, AI robotics, visuo-tactile fusion, defense analytics, multi-INT fusion
